$schema_description: Exchange Profile schema for Coinnect — factual reference data for money service providers

logo_url.description: URL to official logo (press kit or brand page)

regulated_in.description: List of jurisdictions or regulatory bodies

supported_currencies.description: Count or descriptive range of supported currencies

supported_countries.description: Count or descriptive range of supported countries

transfer_methods.description: Methods: bank, card, mobile money, crypto, cash pickup, etc.

fee_model.description: Brief factual description of how fees work

api_docs.description: Public API documentation URL, if available

coinnect_data_source.description: How Coinnect gets pricing data: CCXT order book, direct API, published fees

exchanges.name: Wise
exchanges.fee_model: Transparent variable fee (typically 0.33%–4.75% depending on currency pair) applied to the mid-market exchange rate with no markup. Fee shown upfront before confirmation.
exchanges.speed: 74% of transfers arrive in under 20 seconds; 95% within one day. Some currency pairs may take 1–6 days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2011 by Kristo Kaarmann and Taavet Hinrikus, who were personally frustrated by the cost of converting GBP and EUR.
Serves over 14.8 million customers, businesses, and banks, moving approximately $16 billion monthly.
Listed on the London Stock Exchange since July 2021.
Uses the mid-market exchange rate with no markup — one of the first major providers to do so.
Holds 65 regulatory licenses worldwide, operating in over 80 countries.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Wise public rates API with corridor-specific published fee estimates

exchanges.name: Binance
regulated_in: UAE — ADGM (Recognised Investment Exchange, Recognised Clearing House, Broker-Dealer)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.10% maker/taker, with tiered discounts based on volume and BNB holdings. Fiat deposit/withdrawal fees vary by method and currency.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits typically 1–3 business days via bank transfer, instant via card.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2017 by Changpeng Zhao; grew to become the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume.
Serves over 300 million registered users in more than 180 countries.
Supports over 1,300 cryptocurrencies and 50+ fiat currencies.
First crypto exchange to secure a global license under the ADGM framework (Abu Dhabi, 2026).
Operates Binance Academy, one of the largest free crypto education platforms.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.binance)

exchanges.name: Western Union
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state-level money transmitter licenses in all 50 states
exchanges.fee_model: Fees vary by corridor, amount, delivery method, and payment method. Online transfers tend to have lower fees than in-person agent transactions. Exchange rate includes a margin.
exchanges.speed: Cash pickup available within minutes in many corridors. Bank transfers typically 1–4 business days depending on destination.
interesting_facts: Founded in 1851, Western Union is one of the oldest financial services companies in the world.
Operates a network of approximately 455,000 agent locations across 200+ countries and territories.
Supports transfers in more than 130 currencies.
Originally a telegraph company — sent the first transcontinental telegram in 1861.
Publicly traded on the NYSE under ticker symbol WU.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees and exchange rate estimates (no public rate API for consumer transfers)

exchanges.name: Remitly
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state money transmitter licenses
exchanges.fee_model: Per-transfer fee varies by corridor and delivery speed (Express or Economy). Typical fees range from $1.49–$3.99 per transfer. Exchange rate includes a margin that varies by corridor.
exchanges.speed: Express transfers can arrive in minutes (debit/credit card funding). Economy transfers typically 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2011 by Matt Oppenheimer, Josh Hug, and Shivaas Gulati.
Publicly traded on NASDAQ under ticker symbol RELY since September 2021.
Disbursement network includes 435,000+ cash pickup locations, ~4 billion bank accounts, ~1.1 billion mobile wallets, and 900 million cards.
Supports transfers from 17 sending countries to over 170 receiving countries.
Acquired Rewire (now Remitly for Immigrants) to serve long-term migrant financial needs.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees with open exchange rate API fallback (corridor-specific static estimates)

exchanges.name: OKX
regulated_in: UAE — VARA (Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority, Dubai)
EU — MiCA license (via OKCoin Europe Ltd, Malta)
Singapore — MAS Major Payment Institution license
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.08% maker / 0.10% taker, with tiered discounts based on volume and OKB holdings. P2P trading is fee-free for makers.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes depending on blockchain. Fiat deposits via P2P are typically instant; bank transfers 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2017 by Star Xu; originally launched as OKEx, rebranded to OKX in 2022.
Serves over 50 million users across 100+ countries.
Holds a MiCA-compliant license passported across all 30 EU/EEA member states.
Offers an integrated Web3 wallet with DEX aggregation, NFT marketplace, and DeFi access.
Official sponsor of Manchester City F.C. and the McLaren Formula 1 team.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.okx)

exchanges.name: Kraken
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), Wyoming SPDI charter (Special Purpose Depository Institution)
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.16% maker / 0.26% taker for low volume, decreasing with higher 30-day volume. Fiat deposit/withdrawal fees vary by method (ACH free, wire $5–$35).
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. ACH deposits available in 1–5 business days; wire transfers 1–5 business days. Instant buy available via card.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2011 by Jesse Powell; one of the longest-operating crypto exchanges.
Holds a Wyoming Special Purpose Depository Institution (SPDI) charter — one of the first crypto-native bank charters in the US.
Supports 6 fiat currencies (USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, AUD, CHF) and 430+ cryptocurrencies.
Relocated global headquarters from San Francisco to Cheyenne, Wyoming in 2025.
Licensed as a CASP under MiCA in Ireland, enabling passporting across the EU/EEA.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: CCXT order book (live bid/ask via ccxt.kraken)

exchanges.name: MoneyGram
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), licensed money transmitter in all 50 states
US — Georgia Department of Banking and Finance (NMLS #898432)
exchanges.fee_model: Fees vary by corridor, amount, delivery method, and payment method. The MoneyGram API provides a quote endpoint with send amount, receive amount, fees, and guaranteed FX rate.
exchanges.speed: Cash pickup available within minutes at 400,000+ agent locations. Bank deposits typically 1–3 business days. Mobile wallet transfers often same-day.
interesting_facts: Formed in 1988; one of the largest global money transfer networks alongside Western Union.
Operates approximately 400,000 agent locations in 200+ countries and territories.
Acquired by Madison Dearborn Partners in 2023, becoming a private company after years on NASDAQ.
Founding federated node operator on Midnight, a privacy-focused blockchain network.
Offers a public developer API for partners to integrate money transfer and crypto on/off-ramp services.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees (MoneyGram developer API available for partner integration)

exchanges.name: Payoneer
regulated_in: US — FinCEN (Money Services Business), state money transmitter licenses
EU — Electronic Money Institution (Central Bank of Ireland)
Hong Kong — Money Service Operator license
US — OCC national trust bank charter application filed (Feb 2026, for PAYO Digital Bank, N.A.)
exchanges.fee_model: Cross-border payments in 150+ currencies. Balance withdrawals to local bank incur a fee of up to 2%. Currency conversion fee up to 2% above mid-market rate. Receiving payments from marketplaces is free.
exchanges.speed: Payments between Payoneer accounts are instant. Bank withdrawals typically 2–5 business days depending on destination country and bank.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2005; publicly traded on NASDAQ under ticker symbol PAYO since June 2021.
Serves businesses in over 200 countries, enabling cross-border payments in 150+ currencies.
Account balances available in USD, EUR, GBP, HKD, and JPY with local receiving accounts.
Partner of major global marketplaces including Amazon, Fiverr, Upwork, and Airbnb.
Filed application for a US national trust bank charter (PAYO Digital Bank, N.A.) in February 2026.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees (Payoneer API available for partner payout integration)

exchanges.name: Global66
regulated_in: Chile — CMF (Comision para el Mercado Financiero)
Peru — SBS (Superintendencia de Banca, Seguros y AFP)
Registered in each Latin American market where it operates
exchanges.fee_model: No explicit transfer fee for most international transfers. Revenue comes from a markup on the mid-market exchange rate. Domestic transfers may incur a small fee.
exchanges.speed: Most transfers completed within one business day. Instant transfers between Global66 users.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2018 by Tomas Bercovich and Cristobal Forno in Santiago, Chile.
Operates in 6 Latin American countries (Chile, Colombia, Peru, Mexico, Argentina, Ecuador) with transfers to 80+ destinations.
Mobilized over US$3.7 billion in transfer volume across Latin America in 2025, a 157% year-over-year growth.
Offers a SmartCard (currently available in Chile, Colombia, and Peru) for international purchases and ATM withdrawals.
One of the fastest-growing fintechs in the Latin American cross-border payments space.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Published fees (no public API available; exchange rate markup estimated from pricing page)

exchanges.name: Bitso
regulated_in: Mexico — CNBV (registered Financial Technology Institution under Fintech Law)
First crypto platform in Latin America licensed by the Gibraltar Financial Services Commission
exchanges.fee_model: Spot trading fees start at 0.10%–0.60% maker/taker depending on 30-day volume tier. Fiat deposits via SPEI (Mexico) are free; other deposit methods vary.
exchanges.speed: Crypto deposits confirmed in minutes. SPEI deposits (Mexico) typically within minutes during banking hours. International fiat 1–3 business days.
interesting_facts: Founded in 2014 by Daniel Vogel, Ben Peters, and Pablo Gonzalez; the largest cryptocurrency exchange in Latin America.
First crypto platform in Latin America to receive a DLT license from the Gibraltar Financial Services Commission.
Registered as a Financial Technology Institution (ITF) under Mexico's Fintech Law, supervised by the CNBV.
Supports fiat currencies MXN, ARS, COP, and BRL — enabling localized trading across key Latin American markets.
Offers Bitso Business with APIs for developers to access crypto liquidity and LatAm local payment rails.
exchanges.coinnect_data_source: Direct API (live order book via Bitso REST API — not in CCXT, custom adapter)
